ideation: This animation sequence simulates the action of adjusting a satellite dish, focusing on the precise movements needed for alignment. The robot will use its arms to mimic the careful adjustments required to point the dish accurately.  Frame 1 will show both arms in a neutral resting position. Frame 2 will show the robot's left arm reaching out and slightly upward, as if grasping or guiding the dish's position. Frame 3 will depict a slight adjustment in the left arm's position, indicating a fine-tuning of the dish's angle. Frame 4 will show the left arm returning to a neutral position, implying the alignment is complete. Frame 5 will show both arms returning to a neutral resting position, signaling the end of the adjustment process.  The right arm will remain largely stationary throughout to emphasize the left arm's focused action.

frames.beat: arms rest, ready to adjust the dish
left arm reaches out to guide the dish
fine-tuning the angle with a small adjustment
both arms settle back to rest
